title: potentilla hirta subsp. recta
intro: Potentilla hirta subsp. recta, also known as the hairy cinquefoil, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various skin issues such as eczema, acne, and wounds, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial activities that can help protect against infections and promote tissue repair. Potentilla hirta subsp. recta can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, often combined with other herbs, to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
